buyback_and_burn_program.fees_included_in_burn: Opening fees, closing fees, and winning trade fees

competitors.name: Synthetix Perps
competitors.buyback_mechanism: 100% of trading fees for SNX/sUSD buyback

competitors.kwenta_integration: Kwenta reacquired as frontend (KWENTA token deprecated, 1:17 SNX exchange rate)

peer_ps_multiples_estimated.avantis_vs_peers: AVNT trades at 3.16x P/S, significantly lower than mature peer Hyperliquid at ~12.7x, suggesting either undervaluation or early-stage risk discount

key_findings.summary_1: Avantis launched systematic 30% buyback-and-burn (every 6h) on March 11, 2026, targeting upgrade to 50% by Q2 2026. At current $15M annualized revenue, this generates ~$4.5M annual burn value, equating to 9.5% effective yield to existing AVNT holders via deflationary pressure.
key_findings.summary_2: AVNT trades at 3.16x P/S multiple ($47.4M MC / $15M annualized revenue), significantly cheaper than peer Hyperliquid (~12.7x) and dYdX, reflecting either early-stage discount or execution risk. Buyback-funded yield model mirrors successful SNX and HYPE frameworks.
key_findings.summary_3: Base chain positioning and low fee structure (1 bps maker, 4 bps taker) competitive vs peers; Q2 2026 plans to increase burn rate to 50% (projecting ~$7.5M annual deflationary value) and rollout of staking tiers (fee discounts) suggest protocol-market fit maturation.

data_quality_notes.tvl_caveat: October 2025 figure of $85M TVL is most recent available; actual April 2026 TVL likely higher
data_quality_notes.volume_data: Cumulative volume ($22B lifetime) confirms protocol traction; 24h volume ($12M) reflects current market conditions
data_quality_notes.revenue_estimation: $15M annualized is official from March 2026 buyback announcement; actual daily revenue tracking requires access to real-time on-chain data
data_quality_notes.peer_data_sources: Hyperliquid, dYdX, GMX, SNX data from published reports and governance documents (March-April 2026 timeframe)
